cards.back: A pipeline consists of a series of stages, each of which performs a specific function, such as addition, shift, or load/store
The number of stages in a pipeline, which affects its performance and throughput
The time taken by a stage to complete its operation, typically measured in clock cycles
The time taken to clear the pipeline after a new instruction is issued, typically measured in clock cycles
The percentage of pipeline stages that are occupied by instructions, affecting overall pipeline performance
